data.title: ITS Logistics Port Rail Ramp Index for July 2025 reports stable port operations but uncertainty due to rising project freight imports. US container volume for June increased by 1.8%, Port of Los Angeles had its busiest June with 8% higher volumes than in 2024. Contention, trucking, equipment availability, and distribution centers are at normal level absorption of inbound container flow.
data.description: ITS Logistics released the July forecast for the ITS Logistics US Port/Rail Ramp Freight Index, showing most US ports operating normally but smaller ports facing strain from rising project freight imports. Container volumes hit record highs at the Port of Los Angeles in June, with overall US container volume for June at 2,217,675 TEUs, up 1.8% month-over-month. The Port of Los Angeles saw an 8% increase from 2024 volumes, making it the busiest June on record. However, uncertainties remain due to frontloading trends, tariff volatility, nearshoring efforts, and domestic manufacturing, which could strain specialized equipment capacity for hazmat, oversized, and breakbulk cargo. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Goldman Sachs surpasses Q2 estimates with $14.58B in net revenue, $10.91 EPS, and first-half EPS of $25.07. Analysts increase forecasts.
data.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. (NYSE: GS) exceeded analyst expectations in its second-quarter report, with a 15% increase in net revenue year over year to $14.58 billion. This exceeded the consensus estimate of $13.36 billion but represented a 3% decrease from the previous quarter. GAAP earnings per share stood at $10.91, compared to $8.62 the previous year, surpassing the consensus of $9.48. First-half earnings per share rose to $25.07 from $20.21 the previous year. David Solomon, the Chairman and CEO of Goldman Sachs, noted that the economy and markets are showing positive responses.

data.title: GE Aerospace's Q2 2025 results surpass expectations, with $10.2B adjusted revenue (23% YoY rise), $11.02B GAAP revenue (21% increase), $7.99B CES revenue (30% rise), $2.56B Defense revenue (7% rise), $11.7B CES orders (28%) and $2.9B Defense orders (24%). Adjusted EPS at $1.66 (38% YoY), 23% adjusted operating profit margin. Market reacts positively.
data.description: Shares of GE Aerospace (NYSE: GE) saw an increase in Thursday's trading session following the release of their second-quarter 2025 results. The company reported adjusted revenue of $10.2 billion, representing a 23% year-over-year (YoY) increase, while GAAP revenue stood at $11.02 billion, a 21% rise, surpassing the estimated $9.57 billion. Commercial Engines & Services revenue reached $7.99 billion (+30% YoY), and Defense & Propulsion Technologies revenue amounted to $2.56 billion (+7% YoY). Throughout the quarter, GE Aerospace registered $11.7 billion in Commercial Engines & Services orders, up 28%, with services also increasing by 28%, while Defense & Propulsion Technologies orders rose by 24% YoY to $2.9 billion. Adjusted EPS for the quarter came in at $1.66 (+38% YoY), outperforming the expected $1.41. The adjusted operating profit margin for GE Aerospace contracted by 10 basis points to 23%, with an adjusted operating profit of $2.34 billion, marking a 23% YoY increase.

data.title: Knightscope partners with Palantir to enhance public safety, joining the FedStart program for US federal marketplace.
data.description: In Sunnyvale, California, Knightscope, Inc. (KSCP) has recently signed a two-year deal with Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) to expedite its entry into the U.S. federal marketplace. Knightscope is renowned for its autonomous security robots and artificial intelligence technologies. By becoming part of Palantir’s FedStart program, Knightscope gains access to an accelerated route towards FedRAMP High and DoD Impact Level 5 accredited environment. This collaboration marks a significant step for Knightscope in leveraging Palantir's expertise to enhance its presence in the federal market.

data.title: Tony Marcello appointed as Denver Office Executive by Michael Baker International, with over 40 years of experience.
data.description: A highly experienced professional with over four decades of expertise in transportation design, planning, and construction engineering projects has assumed a new position in Pittsburgh on July 17, 2025. Michael Baker International, a renowned company providing engineering, consulting, and technology services worldwide, welcomes this industry veteran. This individual's wealth of knowledge will undoubtedly contribute significantly to the company's operations, bringing valuable insights acquired from years of hands-on experience.

data.title: Dow Jones up on jobless claims, retail sales; TSMC sees gains post earnings, Nvidia chipmaker also up.
data.description: Thursday saw an increase in the Dow Jones index due to unexpected positive numbers in jobless claims and retail sales. Chipmaker TSMC, affiliated with Nvidia, also experienced a rise in earnings. The stock market presented live coverage, showcasing the Dow's ascent due to the surprising results in jobless claims and retail sales, alongside the positive performance of TSMC, the chipmaker for Nvidia.

data.title: Strata Identity introduces Maverics Identity Orchestration for AI agents with runtime policy-based security mechanisms.
data.description: In Boulder, Colorado, a new development in CloudIAM technology has been introduced. Autonomous AI agents, which function as users but lack identity safeguards and visibility, are a concern in the industry. Strata has unveiled a solution with runtime policy-based security measures to address this issue, providing a safer environment for these agents. This innovation aims to ensure that agents operate within specified parameters to enhance security and control, potentially impacting the industry's approach to AI technology.

data.title: TSG ranks top payment gateway APIs in 2025 with annual API Best of Breed Report powered by GEM platform.
data.description: In Omaha, Nebraska, TSG (The Strawhecker Group), known for its expertise in payments analysis and consultancy, recognizes outstanding companies for their API excellence and innovation. Through the annual API Best of Breed Report, which utilizes TSG’s Global Experience Monitoring (GEM) platform, payment gateways’ APIs are evaluated on various criteria to offer insights, benchmarks, and suggestions to enhance developer integration experience. This assessment offers a comprehensive view into the development processes.

data.title: Diliko recognized as one of top 10 big data tools in 2025 by CRN, transforming data management for businesses.
data.description: Reston, Virginia - An annual list compiled by CRN recognizes the top tools in the industry that are reshaping data management, analysis, and decision-making for businesses. The list highlights the most innovative solutions that are revolutionizing the way organizations handle their data.

data.title: Planet Labs PBC extends contract with IGAC in Colombia, providing satellite data & AI analytics. #Planet #IGAC #Colombia
data.description: A renowned data provider, Planet Labs PBC (NYSE: PL), shared news of a significant contract extension with Instituto Geográfico Agustín Codazzi (IGAC) today. The agreement, facilitated in collaboration with Planet's geospatial intelligence partner Procalculo, entails the utilization of Planet's innovative technology for national land management by IGAC. This extended contract includes the integration of the Planet Insights Platform and an AI-driven change detection service. This move underscores IGAC's commitment to leveraging cutting-edge tools for enhancing its operations.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson surpasses expectations with Q2 earnings, raises FY2025 guidance. Q2 adjusted earnings at $2.77/share, sales at $23.74 billion. FY2025 expected adjusted earnings up to $10.80-$10.90.
data.description: Johnson & Johnson, a pharmaceutical company listed on the NYSE under the ticker JNJ, reported strong quarterly earnings exceeding expectations and upgraded its full-year 2025 earnings outlook. In the second quarter of 2025, the company delivered adjusted earnings of $2.77 per share, a slight decrease of 1.8% from the previous year but surpassing the expected $2.68. Johnson & Johnson's sales for the quarter reached $23.74 billion, marking a 5.8% increase from the previous year and beating the projected $22.85 billion. The company has adjusted its fiscal year 2025 guidance, anticipating adjusted earnings between $10.80 and $10.90, up from the previous estimate of $10.50-$10.70, compared to the expected $10.62. Additionally, Johnson & Johnson raised its sales guidance from $91 billion-$91.8 billion. For the full story, refer to Benzinga.com.

data.title: Omni Family Institute's program earns "Supported" rating from Title IV-E Prevention Services Clearinghouse for trauma-informed care.
data.description: In Nashville, Tennessee on July 17, 2025, Omni Family Institute, a prominent provider of trauma-informed services for individuals affected by the child welfare system, has revealed that their flagship initiative, Together Facing the Challenge, has attained a "Supported" rating. This program is recognized for its evidence-based approach in supporting individuals in need.

data.title: Evergy (NASDAQ: EVRG) to discuss Q2 2025 earnings on August 7, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time (8:00 a.m. Central Time).
data.description: Evergy, Inc. (NASDAQ: EVRG) has announced that it will reveal its second-quarter earnings for 2025 on Thursday, August 7, 2025, before the market opens. The company is scheduled to conduct the quarterly conference call and audio webcast to discuss these results on the same day at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time (8:00 a.m. Central Time). To participate, individuals can access the webcast and presentation slides at the given date and time.

data.title: UroMems gets FDA and ANSM approval for UroActive® Smart Implant study on male SUI, following successful trials in France.
data.description: After demonstrating successful feasibility clinical results in France, UroMems has gained approval for its IDE in both Grenoble, France, and Minneapolis. The company is known for its cutting-edge mechatronics technology designed to address stress urinary incontinence (SUI). This development comes as UroMems continues to make significant strides in the medical field, showcasing the growing interest in its innovative solutions. The approval in both countries highlights the widespread recognition and acceptance of the company's technology, marking a significant milestone in its journey.

data.title: Interactive Brokers (IBKR) will report Q2 earnings on July 17, with estimated EPS of 45 cents and revenue of $1.37 billion. Daily trades saw an increase of 40%. Stock price closed at $59.45.
data.description: Interactive Brokers Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: IBKR) is set to announce its second-quarter earnings after the market closes on Thursday, July 17. Analysts are predicting earnings of 45 cents per share, a slight increase from the 44 cents per share reported in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be around $1.37 billion, up from $1.23 billion a year ago. In June, the company reported 3.448 million daily average revenue trades, marking a significant 40% increase from the previous year. Despite this, Interactive Brokers' shares experienced a slight 0.3% drop, closing at $59.45 on Wednesday. For more details and analysis, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.
